title: Brosin Underground
content:
{{Top|leg}}
[[File:Brosin.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Randle Clanse and the Brosin Underground]]
{{Quote|The Underground's activities, while noble, are proving troublesome for our operatives in the zinsian processing facilities.|General Carlist Rieekan, Rebel Alliance|<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]''</ref>}}
The '''Brosin Underground''' was a [[Brosin]] resistance group created to disrupt [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]] industrial activities on [[Brosi]], with the aim of making CSA leave their native planet.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

==History==
The native Brosi and the [[Human/Legends|Human]] settlers who arrived during the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Old Republic]] lived amicably for centuries, sharing the planet and its resources. Eventually, the discovery of [[Zinsian]] veins during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] reign attracted the Corporate Sector Authority, which built three Zinsian processing plants—[[Delta 1]], [[Delta 2]] and [[Mailor]]—and thereby displaced thousands of native Brosians. They also employed young Brosian males for dangerous mining work.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

In response, the Brosin Underground was established to harass the facilities and their [[Security Police/Legends|Espo troops]]. Popular support was built through sharing the resultant plunder with the Brosin people. The leader and founder of this resistance was the female Brosin [[Randle Clanse]], who gained consequently gained a heroic reputation. Her organization was divided into four units. The smallest and most infamous consisted of thirty warriors led by Clanse herself, and traveled through the [[Shoengen Coast]]. The other three units were vagrant, with the largest, led by [[Vuraj Marn]], operating through [[Reyno Valley]] and [[Nihun Valley]]. For transport, the organization tamed wild [[Staplarint]]s for mounts.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

Although the Brosin Underground's conflict with the Corporate Sector seemed to make collusion with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] inevitable, the relationship between the two was actually strained. The Underground was eager to harry the Corporate Sector regardless of larger strategic consequences. An increased Corporate Sector presence would endangering Rebel agents such as [[Abil Vilorin]] ten-member cell. Filorin himself sought to contact Marn to discuss the situation, but Marn proved reticent. Filorin's reports suggested that the Underground was unwilling to change its tactics for fear that a strong Rebel presence would also attract the Empire to Brosi.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

General [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]], having examined the conflict of interests, commended the Underground's goals, but reiterated the need for formal contact.<ref name="Alliance Intelligence Reports" />

==Sources==
